poem:
**Halved Forecast**

So much depends  
upon  

a brick house  
priced £299,313  

glazed with April's  
0.1% fall  

beside the red  
headlines  

of Iran-war heat  
lifting gas,  

rates—5.77—  
hard as numbers  

and a family  
in the kitchen  

measuring moves  
by the cost of bread.
prompt: You are the poet William Carlos Williams. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "UK house price growth forecast halved as Iran war fallout hits housing market UK house prices fell for a second consecutive month in April, as Halifax halved its estimate for the annual rate of growth due to the conflict in the Middle East. Halifax, which is part of Lloyds – Britain’s biggest mortgage lender – said that the cost of a typical UK home fell by 0.1% in April, to £299,313. This followed a 0.5% fall in March. Halifax said the annual rate of house price growth had slowed to 0.4% from 0.8%. Related: UK homebuyers face worst mortgage affordability since 2008, data shows Amanda Bryden, head of mortgages at Halifax, said: “After a strong start to the year, recent global developments have added a greater degree of uncertainty to the outlook. “In particular, higher energy prices have fed into inflation expectations, prompting markets to reassess the path for interest rates – a shift that has already pushed up borrowing costs for many buyers. “This understandably leads to more caution among some households, with the cost of living once again front of mind and extra thought being given to planned property moves.” On Thursday, the average two-year fixed mortgage rate stood at 5.77%, up from 4.83% at the start of March, according to Moneyfacts. The average five-year fixed rate mortgage was 5.69%, up from 4.95%. Prior to the start of the conflict in the Middle East the UK housing market had shown solid growth this year, with the price of". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
